title: The Shattered Peak
description:
A narrative-driven survival horror created during the Digital Bros Game Academy course. Play as a child lost in the mountains, searching for his family while being pursued by something unknown.

Created during the **Digital Bros Game Academy** course, the project was centered around the theme of **3D Survival**. The game focuses heavily on narrative and storytelling, drawing inspiration from *What Remains of Edith Finch*. Our aim was to evoke a strong emotional response from the player as they experience the unfolding plot.

In the game, you play as a child living in the mountains, searching for his lost family while uncovering secrets that were never meant to be revealed. He must learn to survive in a harsh environment while being pursued by something (or someone) unknown.
longDescription:
## My Role

As **Lead Programmer**, I specialized in **Gameplay and AI**, and implemented the majority of the game's core features. The team consisted of fewer than 10 people in total.

This project taught me a lot about **Unreal Engine 5 and C++**, particularly how to write robust, optimized, clean, maintainable, and well-documented code, while also balancing the use of Blueprints. I researched the best approaches and methodologies to ensure high-quality results. I also developed each feature with a **design-oriented** mindset, ensuring that designers had the flexibility to customize features to **bring their visions to life**.

Additionally, this project gave me valuable experience in collaborating with other "departments", mirroring the structure of a real game studio, which was one of the team's and course's primary goals.

In addition to the core mechanics detailed below, I also contributed to:
- **Player Features** — Movement, sprinting, and stamina management
- **Subsystems** — Various underlying systems to support gameplay functionality
- **UI** — In-game widgets with a diegetic UI style
- **VFX and Camera Effects** — Visual effects and camera interactions
- **Version Control** — Co-managed the Git repository using Git Flow branching strategy

## Personal Considerations

Working on this project was an amazing experience. I loved getting to know so many talented people who shared my interests and dedication. Balancing this project with my regular job was a real challenge, but I'm so glad I took it on. I can't even count how much I've learned during this time, and I'm excited to keep learning more every day.

I won't lie — there were some tough moments, especially trying to meet all the deadlines with limited time, while still delivering results we could be proud of. Some of us gave up all our free time to make this happen, and I feel really lucky to have worked with such dedicated people through all those hours. I look forward to continuing to work with them even after this course is over.
techHighlights: AI creature with dynamic spawn system and danger levels
Audio Manager with designer-customizable SFX parameters
HuntZone subsystem with trigger volumes and trace channels
Enemy logic with C++ state machine and State Tree

codeSnippets.title: Creature AI Behavior
codeSnippets.description: An AI-controlled creature inspired by Slender. It spawns at a variable distance based on danger level, which changes with the player's location and playtime. The spawn area is dynamically adjusted between two concentric circles around the player, using nearby objects to play "hide and seek". The creature despawns if the player moves beyond a maximum distance or after a randomized timer. Looking at the creature triggers sound and visual distortions that smoothly scale with proximity and danger level.

codeSnippets.title: Audio Manager
codeSnippets.description: A GameMode component that handles all in-game SFX. It controls when to play audio cues, their volume, and related parameters based on game conditions — creature presence, distance from the player, and other factors. Audio effects are dynamically responsive to the situation. Designers can customize every detail: spatial or non-spatial playback, location, looping, and many other parameters designed in collaboration with the design team.

codeSnippets.title: HuntZone System
codeSnippets.description: Trigger zones placed throughout the map define areas where the creature can spawn and interact with the player. A subsystem class tracks active zones and ensures the creature spawns only when the player enters these areas. Each zone has a specific danger level that influences spawn mechanics and creature behavior.

codeSnippets.title: Flashlight
codeSnippets.description: The player's main tool for navigating the dark environment. In certain areas and under specific conditions, it reveals important story elements. Only the most observant players will discover all the hidden secrets.

codeSnippets.title: Teddy Bear
codeSnippets.description: The key item to fight off the Creature in later stages when its presence is strongest. Since the main character is a child, his most powerful tool is his trusted companion. When used, the teddy bear repels the Creature and lights up the surroundings for a short time, giving the player a brief moment of relief and a chance to find safety.

role: Lead Programmer, Gameplay & AI - Team of ~8
